rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory-building for the comparative-advertising part of the article, not just as background. In the hypothesis development, the authors rely on Jain and Posavac (2004) for the specific mechanism that negative/avoidance comparative framing increases counterarguing, reduces support arguments, and hurts evaluations. They then test when this prior effect holds by introducing implicit-theory orientation as a moderator and interpret Study 2 results against that benchmark.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Jain and Posavac (2004)'s valenced-comparison findings in comparative advertising and uses them to motivate process predictions. It extends that line by showing the effect is contingent on consumers' lay theories (incremental vs. entity) and theory-confirmation conditions.
